summary: Built Tools/localization-return-files-refresh.py and applied it. Added 288 new entries (16 strings × 18 locales) to bring each per-locale review return file from 356 → 372 entries. Existing entries left untouched so any prior reviewer-supplied metadata is preserved. Refreshed all derived artifacts: LQA report (Tools/localization-lqa.py --apply --write-md), dashboard (Tools/localization-review-status.py --write-doc), German packet (Tools/german-review-packet-regenerate.py), all-locale export (Tools/localization-review-export.py). LQA across 18 locales × 372 entries: 6642 passed / 54 warning / 0 reverted. localization-parity unchanged at 19/330/13.
missing_proof_levels: running-app-smoke for translated content per locale

contract_status_changes.before: Per-locale return files were a snapshot of 356 entries from the 2026-05-15 ingest. 16 keys had been added to Localizable.strings since then (2 Check-in/Check in, 8 audio/voice accessibility, 6 interpolation format-strings). The dashboard and LQA reports counted the stale 356 figure across 18 locales.
contract_status_changes.after: All 18 per-locale return files now hold 372 entries each. New entries carry today's review_date (2026-05-16), the same reviewer (claude-opus-4-7 LLM), and status needs-layout-check / keep-english-term following the same rules as the prior ingest. Existing entries left untouched. Dashboard (STATUS.md), LQA report (LQA.md), German packet, and all-locale export all refreshed.
proof: 18 per-locale return files now 372 entries each
STATUS.md / LQA.md / translation-review-export.{csv,json} / german-review-packet.{csv,json} all current

residual_risks: The 16 newly-appended-per-locale entries inherit needs-layout-check / keep-english-term status with no manual review. They are LLM-drafted and unverified. Native review still outstanding for every locale.

Tools/localization-return-files-refresh.py is append-only by design — it does NOT update existing entries if their reviewed_value drifts away from the current resource value. A future divergence detection slice could compare each existing entry against current resources and flag mismatches.

review_date on the new entries is 2026-05-16; existing entries keep 2026-05-15. The dashboard shows the file-level provenance.review_date, not per-entry dates, so this divergence is invisible at the summary level. The lqa_run_date on each file is now 2026-05-16.
The refresh tool's new entries set post_packet_addition: true to mark them as later-added. Downstream consumers should respect that flag when sorting or filtering.
Native review remains outstanding for every locale. app-localization-native-review-intake is still blocked. The dashboard correctly reports 0 native-reviewed locales.
recommended_next_reason: All NLS implementation and housekeeping work is complete. Only blocked slice remaining is app-localization-native-review-intake (needs human-supplied review). Clean stop.
